            • Extension Assistant, Department of Plant Sciences: UTIA
              • Locations
                Knoxville, TN, United States
              • Posting Date
                07/02/2026
              Be the First to Apply

              This position will work on educational programming including content development; organization, planning and execution of workshops and demonstrations; and development of asynchronous course content management. This role will be responsible for oversight in extension programming towards improving production practices especially irrigation, and pest management in nursery crops in Tennessee. The person in this position will also coordinate applied research and demonstrations in greenhouse, nursery, and RECs. This is a hands-on position requiring work outdoors.

            • Communications Specialist - Finance & Administration and Human Resources - UTK
              • Locations
                Knoxville, TN, United States
              • Posting Date
                07/02/2026

              The University of Tennessee, Knoxville (UTK) invites applications for a Communications Specialist to support the Finance & Administration (F&A) and Human Resources (HR) Departments. This position will provide day-to-day communication support to the Strategic Project Manager of HR with social media management, content creation, internal and external communications, print media, and promotion of department initiatives.

            • Medical Records Coordinator-OB/GYN (Knoxville)
              • Locations
                Knoxville, TN, United States
              • Posting Date
                07/02/2026

              Under the direction of an assigned supervisor, the Medical Records Coordinator ensures medical records compliance.

              • Locations

              • Posting Date

            • Academic Program Coordinator - Tombras School of Advertising and Public Relations - CCI- - UTK
              • Locations
                Knoxville, TN, United States
              • Posting Date
                07/02/2026
              Be the First to Apply

              The Academic Program Coordinator is a highly public-facing role responsible for managing the daily front-office workflows, course scheduling logistics, school-level programs and events, and student program support for the Tombras School. This position serves as the primary front-line contact and coordinator for undergraduate and graduate students, student organizations, competition teams, fellowship programs, and faculty logistical workflows. Additionally, this role coordinates general room bookings and collaborates with college and university-level offices to ensure smooth daily operations.

            • Not finding what you're looking for? Join our Talent Community to show interest in our organization and sign up for job alerts.

              Join Our Talent Community
            • Administrative Specialist - Tombras School of Advertising and Public Relations - CCI- UTK
              • Locations
                Knoxville, TN, United States
              • Posting Date
                07/02/2026
              Be the First to Apply

              The Administrative Specialist serves as the primary strategic partner, financial coordinator, and executive assistant to the School Director. This position acts as the vital liaison between the Tombras School and the CCI Office of Finance, Administration, and Operations (OFAO). This role oversees internal budget allocations, verifies compliance for all school-level expenditures, manages executive scheduling, and initiates personnel/hiring workflows prior to submission to the centralized college office.

            • Financial Associate: Department of Theatre - UTK
              • Locations
                Knoxville, TN, United States (On-site)
              • Posting Date
                07/02/2026
              Be the First to Apply

              The University of Tennessee Department of Theatre and Clarence Brown Theatre seeks an Accounting Specialist to provide financial management spanning the academic unit as well as the public-facing, revenue-generating Clarence Brown Theatre. The Accounting Specialist will play a crucial role in assisting the business manager to ensure that revenue and expenses are properly accounted for so that all required reporting to various agencies is accurate and timely.

            • Grants & Contracts Specialist - Baker School - UTK
              • Locations
                Knoxville, TN, United States
              • Posting Date
                07/02/2026
              Be the First to Apply

              The Grants & Contracts Specialist serves as the Baker School and Institute of American Civics (IAC) subject-matter authority for sponsored projects administration and contract coordination. This position provides senior-level expertise and independent compliance oversight across the full award lifecycle, ensuring that sponsored awards, contractual agreements, and restricted funding activities are administered in compliance with federal, state, sponsor, and university requirements.

            • Director of Advancement, Gary W. Rollins College of Business
              • Locations
                Chattanooga, TN, United States
              • Posting Date
                07/02/2026
              Be the First to Apply

              The University of Chattanooga Foundation seeks to fill the position of Director of Development for the Gary W. Rollins College of Business at the University of Tennessee Chattanooga. The Gary W. Rollins College of Business at the University of Tennessee at Chattanooga was named after receiving the largest philanthropic gift in the history of UTC from Gary W. Rollins and Kathleen Rollins in 2018.

              BI Developer delivering scalable Power Platform + Microsoft Fabric solutions—data models, dashboards, and automation—with strong governance, performance tuning, and stakeholder partnership.

            • Consultant Standardized Patient- Simulation Center (Knoxville)
              • Locations
                Knoxville, TN, United States
              • Posting Date
                07/01/2026

              The Consultant- Standardized Patients (SP) are needed to serve in the role of a patient undergoing a physical examination by a student in the health care professions. The SP will simulate the response of an individual with a specified medical condition. The details of the medical condition and the required response by the SP are provided by University professors and instructors.

            • Medicolegal Death Investigator- Forensic Center
              • Locations
                United States
              • Posting Date
                07/01/2026
              Be the First to Apply

              The Medicolegal Death Investigator assists with the investigation of deaths reported to the West Tennessee Regional Forensic Center, Office of the Medical Examiner, particularly as it pertains to the seeking, gathering, and documentation of pertinent circumstantial, historical, and other medicolegal data.

              • Locations

              • Posting Date

              Be the First to Apply

              The University of Tennessee Health Science Center, Department of Medicine Cardiology, is actively seeking applicants for a part-time non-tenured Assistant Professor and Program Director.

            • Extension Specialist I, 4H Youth Development - UTIA
              • Locations
                Knoxville, TN, United States
              • Posting Date

              The Extension Specialist provides statewide leadership for Tennessee 4-H recognition programs, including youth achievement, judging events, scholarships, and major award processes.

            • Assistant Coach, Women's Basketball - UT Southern
              • Locations
                Pulaski, TN, United States
              • Posting Date
                07/01/2026

              Assistant coach will support the head coach with all day to day activities to run the women's basketball program including but not limited to facilitating practices, recruiting high quality student athletes and event day management.

            • Mental Health Therapist - Student Counseling Center -UTK
              • Locations
                Knoxville, TN, United States
              • Posting Date
                07/01/2026

              The Student Counseling Center (SCC) is a multi-disciplinary clinical team providing triage, crisis, short-term therapy, group therapy, consultation, outreach, and campus mental health support. Mental Health Therapists are exempt, year-round, full-time; non-tenure track staff in the Student Counseling Center, who are part of a multi-disciplinary clinical team providing both clinical services and, as appropriate to experience level, supervision for the center’s graduate student trainees.

            • Extension Agent, 4-H Youth Development, Loudon County, UT Extension: UTIA
              • Locations
                Loudon, TN, United States (On-site)
              • Posting Date
                07/01/2026
              Be the First to Apply

              UT Extension delivers programs to youth and adults in all 95 counties of Tennessee. As a member of our team you would join over 100 subject matter specialists and more than 300 agents to address priority issues through Agriculture and Natural Resources, Family and Consumer Sciences, 4-H or Resource Development programs.

            • Parking Maintenance - Parking Services - UTK
              • Locations
                Knoxville, TN, United States
              • Posting Date
                07/01/2026

              The University of Tennessee is seeking dependable and detail-oriented individuals to join our Parking Maintenance team. This position is responsible for ensuring a clean, safe, and welcoming environment across all parking facilities. A Parking Maintenance Worker performs a full range of duties related to parking garage/lot maintenance and minor landscape maintenance, and repairs. This position supports parking operations during on-campus events; signage placement, and placement of other materials. Due to events occurring sporadically, this position must be flexible as hours may adjust based on the needs of the University.

            • Building Services Supervisor - Facilities Services - UTK
              • Locations
                Knoxville, TN, United States
              • Posting Date
                07/01/2026
              Be the First to Apply

              This position is fully trained in all aspects of general cleaning. This position is fully able to do carpet shampooing, hard floor maintenance, recycling, lavatory cleaning, office cleaning, and all routine cleaning procedures. The position is skilled in training new employees, inspecting buildings, and is computer literate. This employee oversees a 24/7 building operation and directs the staff in all Building Service operations of the building. This employee assigns work to foremen and employees, schedules project work, and schedules routine work. This position is the key contact for the building manager and assists in preparing the building for functions, events, and special events.

            • Building Services Assistant Foreman II - Facilities Services - UTK
              • Locations
                Knoxville, TN, United States
              • Posting Date
                07/01/2026
              Be the First to Apply

              The Building Services Assistant Foreman is fully trained in all aspects of general cleaning and is able to perform carpet shampooing, hard floor maintenance, recycling removal, lavatory cleaning, office cleaning, and all other routine cleaning procedures. The position holder should also be skilled in training new employees, inspecting buildings for cleanliness, and must be computer literate.

            • Building Services Aide - Facilities Services - UTK
              • Locations
                Knoxville, TN, United States
              • Posting Date
                07/01/2026

              This position is responsible for the general cleaning of offices, restrooms, public traffic areas, and waste and recycling removal. This role requires performing general physical activities that require considerable use of your arms and legs and moving your whole body, such as climbing, lifting, balancing, walking, stooping, and handling materials. The individual occupying this role is responsible for developing a plan to prioritize, organize, and accomplish their work and must be able to work occasional weekends to support home football games and other special events.

            • Building Services Foreman - Facilities Services - UTK
              • Locations
                Knoxville, TN, United States
              • Posting Date
                07/01/2026
              Be the First to Apply

              The Building Services Foreman serves as an on-duty supervisor within the Building Services unit and is responsible for supervising up to 30 employees. This role is responsible for ensuring that adequate supplies are in inventory, that equipment is in working order, for overseeing building cleanliness according to APPA standards, scheduling employee coverage, and scheduling project work. In addition, the foreman must submit daily reports of time spent by each employee in each area, communicate with customers, and initiate tiny trash and total cleaning sustainability goals. FML reports, injury reports, vehicle accident reports, as well as annual training fall under the foreman’s responsibility.

            • Kitchen Technician - Facilities Services - UTK
              • Locations
                Knoxville, TN, United States
              • Posting Date
                06/30/2026
              Be the First to Apply

              The Kitchen Technician will collaborate closely with restaurant and hospitality managers to devise and implement all-encompassing service solutions tailored to the intricacies of commercial cooking and food service equipment. This person will engage in multifaceted maintenance and repair tasks spanning a diverse array of industrial kitchen apparatus, encompassing deep fryers, ovens, tray returns, grills, refrigeration units, bun toasters, steamers, slicers, hot holding cabinets, dish machines, coffee makers, and a plethora of other specialized culinary equipment.

            • Building Project Manager: Haslam College of Business - UTK
              • Locations
                Knoxville, TN, United States
              • Posting Date
                06/30/2026

              The Haslam College of Business (HCB) at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville, invites applications for a Building Project Manager (Exempt, Full Time) to join our Dean’s Office.

            • Assistant Professor - The Hamilton Eye Institute Department of Ophthalmology
              • Locations
                Memphis, TN, United States
              • Posting Date
                06/30/2026
              Be the First to Apply

              The Hamilton Eye Institute Department of Ophthalmology at the University of Tennessee Health Science Center is seeking full time Pediatric Ophthalmologist and Strabismus Specialist. This is a non-tenure-track position at the level of Instructor, Assistant, Associate, or full Professor. Appointment rank will be commensurate with academic accomplishments and experience.

            • Interactive Learning - Instructional Designer & Developer - Digital Learning - UTK
              • Locations
                Knoxville, TN, United States
              • Posting Date
                06/30/2026
              Trending

              The University of Tennessee is seeking an Instructional Designer and Developer for our Interactive Learning team to support the design, development, and implementation of engaging online and hybrid learning experiences. This position will primarily function as an Interactive Learning Object (ILO) Developer, working closely with faculty, media teams, and learning technology staff to build interactive instructional content. The person in this position will help the university meet Chancellor Plowman's strategic goal of expanding educational opportunities beyond the Knoxville campus through the development and delivery of robust and engaging online programs and courses.

              The University of Tennessee, Knoxville is seeking a Network Services Admin II to design, implement, support, and maintain a highly secure, resilient, and high-performance wired and wireless network infrastructure that empowers academic research, educational excellence, and seamless university operations.

            • Assistant Business Manager: Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering - UTK
              • Locations
                Knoxville, TN, United States (On-site)
              • Posting Date
                06/30/2026

              Reporting to the Principal Business Manager the Assistant Business Manager in the Civil and Environmental Engineering Department within the Tickle College of Engineering supports departmental financial operations and human resources functions by ensuring accurate processing of financial transactions and maintaining compliance with university and agency policies.

            • Chief Operating Officer: Tickle College of Engineering - UTK
              • Locations
                Knoxville, TN, United States (On-site)
              • Posting Date
                06/30/2026

              The Chief Operating Officer (COO) serves as a member of the Tickle College of Engineering Dean’s senior leadership team. This role provides strategic expertise and guidance to include managing internal operations and special projects, assessing performance and giving feedback, coordinating communication between internal units, managing process improvement across the TCE, managing relationships with internal and external partners, giving strategic advice to the Dean, and acting as a surrogate for the Dean when unavailable.

            • Open Rank Professor– Oral and Maxillofacial Pathology
              • Locations
                Memphis, TN, United States
              • Posting Date
                06/30/2026
              Be the First to Apply

              The University of Tennessee Health Science Center College of Dentistry invites applications for a full-time non-tenure-track faculty at the rank of Assistant, Associate, or Full Professor in Oral and Maxillofacial Pathology. The successful candidate will join the Department of Diagnostic Sciences and contribute to the department's missions of excellence in education, patient care, research, and service.

            • Assistant Director of Regional Engagement
              • Locations
                Chattanooga, TN, United States
              • Posting Date
                06/30/2026

              The Assistant Director of Regional Engagement is responsible for planning and staffing alumni events, building and maintaining relationships with alumni and community constituents, and identifying opportunities for meaningful engagement with the university.

            • Associate Division Planner - IPS
              • Locations
                Knoxville, TN, United States
                and 3 more
              • Posting Date
                06/29/2026
              Be the First to Apply

              In this new position, the Associate Division Planner is responsible for performing complex and highly responsible professional planning activities for the University of Tennessee’s Institute of Public Service, Center for Local Planning. Work will occur in cooperation with local partners in a specific Grand Division of the State. The position carries out technical assignments including project management, research, data analysis, oral presentations, and development of policy and plan recommendations. The role requires professionalism, tact, and diplomacy in working with a wide range of stakeholders. Reports to the Senior Division Planner.

              • Locations
                Knoxville, TN, United States (On-site)
              • Posting Date

              The Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science (EECS) at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville, invites applications for a full-time Student Services Associate. Reporting to the Front Office Manager, this position serves as a key point of contact for graduate students, faculty, and prospective applicants.

              The Division of Student Life at the University of Tennessee invites applications for the Administrative Support Assistant (Office Manager) position for Student Conduct & Community Standards (SCCS). This is a 12-month, full-time, staff position.

            • Senior Herd Caretaker, Plateau AgResearch and Education Center: UTIA
              • Locations
                Crossville, TN, United States
              • Posting Date
                06/29/2026
              Be the First to Apply

              Position is responsible for the care and welfare of livestock by observing and reporting any possible problems related to animal welfare. Position cleans and maintains work environment/facilities. Feeding, watering, taking measurements, recording research data, care and cleaning of equipment. Maintains animal health programs and performs regular vaccination programs. Performs farm labor in support of ag operations and hay/feed production.

            • Public Records Coordinator, Division of Communications and Marketing - UT System Administration
              • Locations
                Knoxville, TN, United States
              • Posting Date
                06/29/2026

              The University of Tennessee System is the state’s oldest higher education institution and preeminent public university. The UT System is comprised of campuses at Knoxville, Chattanooga, Pulaski and Martin; the Health Science Center at Memphis; and the statewide Institute of Agriculture and Institute for Public Service. With a presence in each of Tennessee’s 95 counties and through the combined force of education, research and outreach, the University serves students, business and industry, schools, governments, organizations and residents statewide.

            • Temporary Health & Education Policy Research Associate: College of Nursing - UTK
              • Locations
                Knoxville, TN, United States
              • Posting Date
                06/29/2026
              Be the First to Apply

              The College of Nursing (CON) at the University of Tennessee Knoxville, the state’s flagship research institution, seeks a health and education policy research associate to join an interdisciplinary research team under the direct mentorship of Clea McNeely, DrPH.

            • Testing Specialist – Student Disability Services - UTK
              • Locations
                Knoxville, TN, United States
              • Posting Date
                06/29/2026

              Reporting to the Testing Coordinator, the Testing Specialist performs administrative and proctoring duties to assist in the daily operations of the Student Disability Services (SDS) Testing Center.

            • Medical Director, Student Health Center - UTK
              • Locations
                Knoxville, TN, United States
              • Posting Date
                06/29/2026
              Be the First to Apply

              Organizes, administers, and directs all medical activities of the Student Health Center (SHC), including administering and directing the professional activities of all staff Physicians, Physical Therapists, and Nurse Practitioners; the administration of the Laboratory and Radiology Departments of the Health Center; and provision of outpatient medical care in an ambulatory setting. Working in partnership with the Assistant Vice Chancellor for Student Life and/or Health Care Administrator, the role supports coordinated, efficient Student Health Center operations.

            • Landscape Services Foreman - Facilities Services - UTK
              • Locations
                Knoxville, TN, United States
              • Posting Date
                06/29/2026
              Be the First to Apply

              The Facilities Services Department is responsible for the maintenance and operations of all campus facilities, which includes buildings, grounds, and utility systems. The Landscape Foreman is a working supervisor and trainer assigned to a crew providing landscape maintenance for the campus.

            • Post-Doctoral Research Associate: Department of Microbiology - UTK
              • Locations
                Knoxville, TN, United States (On-site)
              • Posting Date
                06/29/2026

              The Clasen lab in the Department of Microbiology at UTK is seeking a post-doctoral researcher with expertise in structural biology. The lab studies the relationship between the host immune system and the commensal microbes that colonize the intestinal tract. We seek to understand how host immune responses impact microbial behavior. To address this, we employ a combination of techniques in molecular biology and immunology, next generation sequencing, and in vitro and in vivo models.

            • Engaged Online Course Coordinator - Digital Learning - UTK
              • Locations
                Knoxville, TN, United States
              • Posting Date
                06/29/2026

              Reporting to the Executive Director of Online Programs, the Engaged Online Course (EOC) Coordinator on team regularly interacts with faculty, academic units, and others in DL to promote high quality, evidence-based practices in online teaching, learning, and assessment at the University. The person in this position will help the university meet Chancellor Plowman's strategic goal of expanding educational opportunities beyond the Knoxville campus through the development and delivery of robust and engaging online programs and courses.

            • Principal Business Manager: Center for Transportation Research - UTK
              • Locations
                Knoxville, TN, United States (On-site)
              • Posting Date
                06/29/2026

              The Principal Business Manager leads financial management, budgeting processes, human resources administration, business operations, and data reporting for the unit. The position ensures the accuracy, consistency, and integrity of all financial activities, coordinates sponsored research budgeting and post award financial management, and provides guidance to faculty, staff, and leadership regarding fiscal and operational decisions. The role maintains internal controls, advises leadership on strategic initiatives, supports compliance, oversee staff performance, manages space planning, and coordinates the acquisition and analysis of critical data for reporting, audits, accreditation, and academic program review.

            • SR. Maintenance Specialist I - Facilities Services - UTK
              • Locations
                Knoxville, TN, United States
              • Posting Date
                06/29/2026
              Be the First to Apply

              The Senior Maintenance Specialist within the One Call unit repairs and performs maintenance on all mechanical/electrical within the envelope of buildings and perimeter. One Call is responsible for answering and reacting to customers’ needs and concerns 24 hours a day, 365 days a year. This is a specialty group whose primary focus is customer service by answering the One Call phone in a professional manner, interpreting customer’s needs, performing or facilitating necessary repairs with an excellent customer satisfaction outcome. This group performs any repairs required by customers to include lighting, ceilings, doors and hardware, restrooms, general plumbing, etc. The position requires the ability to follow all repairs through to completion including repairs that are performed by other Facilities Services units while maintaining contact with the customer keeping them informed of progress.

            • CIRCLE Research Project Coordinator - College of Education, Health and Human Sciences - UTK
              • Locations
                Knoxville, TN, United States
              • Posting Date
                06/29/2026

              This position coordinates and manages the implementation of research-focused initiatives and associated research programming between the University of Tennessee (UT) and River Valley Health (RVH).

            • Associate Director of Digital Strategy
              • Locations
                Knoxville, TN, United States
              • Posting Date
                06/29/2026

              The Associate Director of Digital Strategy is responsible for the day-to-day management, optimization, and execution of Advancement web initiatives while also serving as a strategic partner in identifying opportunities, recommending solutions, and shaping digital experiences that advance divisional goals and campaign priorities.
